title: Travere wins long-awaited approval for kidney disease drug
summary: The clearance of Filspari in a condition known as FSGS ends a winding regulatory journey and unlocks a revenue opportunity believed to be worth more than $1 billion annually.

title: J&J leans on Tremfya, cancer drugs to overcome Stelara losses
summary: A growing multiple myeloma franchise helped the pharmaceutical giant top Wall Street’s expectations and surpass $15 billion in first-quarter drug sales.

title: Lilly boosts ADC portfolio with CrossBridge Bio deal
summary: Worth up to $300 million, the buyout is Lilly’s latest of an ADC maker and adds technology for making “dual-payload” therapies.

title: Novo teams with OpenAI; Ideaya gets muted response to eye drug data
summary: Novo intends to “fully integrate” AI capabilities across its operations. Elsewhere, Regeneron made its first foray into radiopharmaceuticals and a GSK cancer drug showed early promise.

title: Spyre drug for inflammatory bowel disease shows promise in early study
summary: The therapy, which works similarly to Takeda’s blockbuster Entyvio, showed remission rates that suggest a “best-in-class” profile, the company said.

title: Allogene data suggest ‘off-the-shelf’ CAR-T could delay relapse in lymphoma
summary: Initial results from a high-stakes trial indicate the biotech may have finally found a niche in lymphoma care for a donor-derived cell therapy.

title: Revolution pancreatic cancer drug nearly doubles survival in key trial
summary: The subject of buyout rumors this year, Revolution posted Phase 3 results one analyst called a “game changer” in a tough-to-treat tumor.

title: Connected medical devices: Smarter care starts here
summary: Connected medical devices are transforming healthcare and clinical trials, driving advances in patient care and next-gen therapies shaping the future.

title: Rethinking dermatology trial design for late-stage success
summary: A smarter approach to dermatology trials—built for durability and real-world impact.

title: FDA again spurns Replimune melanoma drug
summary: In a statement late Friday, CEO Sushil Patel disputed the FDA’s criticisms of the company and said Replimune had received “inconsistent communications.” It’ll be forced to cut jobs as well.

title: CDER Small Business and Industry Assistance (SBIA) Learn
summary: SBIA Learn offers a variety of multimedia resources to provide information that is comprehensive, interactive, and easily accessible to small pharmaceutical business and industry.

title: Drug Compliance Programs
summary: FDA compliance programs provide guidance and instructions to FDA staff for obtaining information to help fulfill agency plans in the specified program area.

title: Search for Pharmaceutical Quality Documents
summary: FDA provides resources on pharmaceutical quality topics, including information on regulations, guidance documents, and compliance programs in a searchable and filterable table format

title: CDER SBIA Chronicles
summary: FDA/CDER SBIA Chronicles, the bi-monthly newsletter of the CDER Small Business and Industry Assistance team, provides industry with useful information to assist in all aspects of drug marketing and regulation

title: Helpful Webinars and Other Resources for Generic Drug Manufacturers
summary: FDA develops resources – workshops, webinars, and seminars – to help generic drug manufacturers improve the quality of their abbreviated new drug application (ANDA) submissions and decrease the number of assessment cycles needed for approval.

title: About the Quantitative Medicine Center of Excellence (QM CoE)
summary: To ensure the CoE is equipped to carry out its mission, the CoE will be governed by leadership from the Office of Generic Drugs, Office of New Drugs, Office of Pharmaceutical Quality, and Office of Translational Sciences.

title: Generic Drug Research Priorities & Projects
summary: In accordance with the Generic Drug User Fee Amendments (GDUFA), FDA consults with industry and the public to create an annual list of regulatory science initiatives on generic drugs. These priorities are discussed and developed in public meetings and workshops, resulting in several awarded projects

title: Generic Drugs in the 21st Century: FDA’s Actions Create Transparency and Value for Complex Generic Product Development
